title: Agent Deadlocks
concept: Identifying cases where two agents are stuck waiting on each other's outputs, and resolving them with timeout resets.

exercises.prompt: What is a deadlock in simple terms?

options.text: A situation where two agents stop working because they are waiting on each other.
A method to improve communication between agents.
A type of agent that never stops working.

exercises.explain: A deadlock occurs when two agents are unable to proceed because they are waiting for each other.

cards.title: What is an Agent Deadlock?
cards.body: An agent deadlock happens when two agents are both waiting for each other to finish something. It’s like a game of tug-of-war where neither team lets go!

cards.title: Definition of Deadlock
cards.body: A deadlock is a situation where two or more agents stop working because they are each waiting for the other to release a resource or provide output.

cards.title: Analogy: Stuck in the Traffic
cards.body: Imagine two cars trying to cross an intersection at the same time. If they both wait for the other to move, they will be stuck indefinitely—just like agents in a deadlock!

cards.title: Example of Agent Deadlock
cards.body: Agent A is waiting for Agent B's response to complete a task, while Agent B is waiting for Agent A to do the same. Neither can move forward.

cards.title: Myth: Deadlocks Aren't Serious Issues
cards.body: Some think that deadlocks are minor glitches. However, they can halt entire processes and need to be resolved quickly!

cards.title: Identifying a Deadlock
cards.body: Look for situations where agents are waiting on each other without making progress.

exercises.prompt: What is one way to resolve an agent deadlock?

options.text: Reset one or both agents after a timeout.
Ignore the situation and wait longer.
Force one agent to finish first.

exercises.explain: Resetting one or both agents helps to break the deadlock and allows them to try again.

exercises.prompt: True or False: In a deadlock, agents can eventually resolve the situation without any intervention.
exercises.explain: In a deadlock, agents typically cannot resolve the situation on their own without intervention such as resetting.

exercises.prompt: Complete the sentence: A deadlock is when two agents are waiting for each other's __________.
exercises.sentence: A deadlock is when two agents are waiting for each other's output.
exercises.explain: In a deadlock, agents are stuck waiting for each other's output, preventing them from making progress.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following scenarios exemplifies a deadlock?

options.text: Agent X needs information from Agent Y before proceeding, while Agent Y is waiting on Agent X's approval.
Agent A finishes its task and informs Agent B to start.
Agent C requests data, and Agent D prepares to send it.

exercises.explain: Scenario 1 shows a classic deadlock where both agents are dependent on each other to proceed.

exercises.prompt: How can an agent deadlock impact a system's performance?

options.text: It can cause the system to operate faster.
It can halt processes entirely until resolved.
It helps agents work better together.
It has no effect on performance.

exercises.explain: A deadlock can stop the entire system from functioning, as the agents involved can't do anything until the deadlock is resolved. Recognizing and handling deadlocks is essential for efficient operation.
